headline: Polytechnic Graduates Outperform University Graduates in Employer Satisfaction
context: 2025 employer survey shows higher satisfaction with polytechnic graduates' practical skills compared to degree holders.

cards.big: Employer satisfaction rate is 78% for polytechnic graduates versus 64% for degree holders
cards.sub: Practical training orientation gives polytechnic alumni immediate workplace readiness

cards.big: Polytechnic programs require minimum 30% hands-on practical training hours since 2015
cards.sub: University programs average only 12% practical component in non-professional fields

cards.big: Despite higher satisfaction polytechnic graduates earn RM600 less monthly than degree holders
cards.sub: Credential hierarchy overrides demonstrated competency in salary negotiations

cards.big: Germany's polytechnic equivalents achieve salary parity with universities through dual systems
cards.sub: Industry co-designed qualifications carry equal labor market value

cards.big: Paying more for less competent graduates because they hold degrees is irrational credentialism
Should employer satisfaction data be published alongside salary data for all programs
